sourceMovie.title: Open Season
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Open Season (2006) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Over the Hedge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Open Season clicked for you, Over the Hedge (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Open Season clicked for you, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Open Season?
faq.answer: Try Over the Hedge, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit, Zootopia 2—each shares DNA with Open Season while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Open Season?
faq.answer: Open Season (2006) is best described as Animation, Family, Adventure, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Open Season part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Open Season Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
